key_themes: Record orders, $190B backlog, and strong engine/aftermarket demand
Bullish Wall Street analysts and price targets above $350
Defense opportunities and post-spin-off value creation
Short-term pre-earnings profit-taking at premium valuation
summary: Sentiment around GE Aerospace (GE) is solidly positive ahead of its April 21, 2026 Q1 earnings. The company recently raised its 2026 operating profit guidance by $1 billion to $9.85–10.25B after a strong Q4 beat, backed by record orders (up 74% YoY), a ~$190B backlog, commercial aviation recovery, aftermarket services, and defense tailwinds. Wall Street remains bullish with meaningful upside in price targets despite a recent 4–5% pullback from profit-taking and high valuation (~37x earnings). Long-term, the GE breakup is widely viewed as a major shareholder value creator.
sources: 10+ recent news articles (raised guidance, earnings previews, analyst commentary), 20+ X posts (earnings buzz, bullish calls, order growth mentions), StockTwits sentiment tracking, Reddit r/stocks investor threads
